---
title: "data availability r shiny app"
author: "Nebiyou Mengistu"
date: "2026-06-05"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(shinyjs)
library(shinythemes)
library(shiny)
library(tidyverse)
```


```{r}
# this part makes the actual categories filterable

metadata_inventory <- tibble(
  Topic = c("Special Education Child Count", "School Lunch/Breakfast Enrollment", 
            "Suicide Mortality Rates", "Youth Mental Health Screenings"),
  
  Category = c("Education", "Youth Well-being", "Youth Health", "Environment"),
  
  Organization = c("Virginia Department of Education (VDOE)", "VDOE / USDA", 
                   "Virginia Department of Health (VDH)", "VDH / Local School Divisions"),
  
  Geographic_Level = c("School Division", "School Division", "County", "Health District"),
  
  Years_Available = c("2019, 2020, 2021, 2022", "2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024", 
                      "2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022", "2022, 2023"),
  
  API_Available = c("Yes (CKAN Portal)", "No (Manual Download)", "No (Static Tables)", "No"),
  
  Status = c("Audited - Ready to Clean", "Identified - Needs Download", 
             "Identified - Needs Download", "Scoping Feasibility")
)

# Save this small file — this acts as your "database" for the dashboard
saveRDS(metadata_inventory, "metadata_inventory.rds")
```


```{r}
# 1. THE USER INTERFACE (UI)
ui = navbarPage("DSPG",
                
  tabPanel("Overview",
    h3("Title of your project"),
    
    # Using a sidebar layout to split inputs from the data display
    sidebarLayout(
      
      # Step 1: Sidebar panel containing the user controls
      sidebarPanel(
        selectInput("filter_category", "Select Category:",
                    choices = c("All Categories", unique(metadata_inventory$Category))),
        
        textInput("search_text", "Search by Text:", placeholder = "e.g., VDOE, Suicide")
      ),
      
      # Step 2: Main panel displaying our output table
      mainPanel(
        h4("Data Availability Grid"),
        tableOutput("inventory_table")
      )
    )
  ),
  
  tabPanel("Lit Review")
)

```

```{r}
# 2. THE BACKEND SERVER LOGIC
server = function(input, output) {
  
  # Step 3: Create a reactive data stream that filters based on UI inputs
  filtered_data <- reactive({
    df <- metadata_inventory
    
    # If the user selects a specific category, filter the rows
    if (input$filter_category != "All Categories") {
      df <- df %>% filter(Category == input$filter_category)
    }
    
    # If the user types text, search across the Topic column
    if (input$search_text != "") {
      df <- df %>% filter(str_detect(tolower(Topic), tolower(input$search_text)))
    }
    
    df
  })
  
  # Step 4: Take the reactive data and render it as a simple table
  output$inventory_table <- renderTable({
    filtered_data()
  })
  
}
```

```{r}
shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)
```
